response:
Short answer
- The 502s were App Gateway (AGW) Bad Gateway responses during short, concentrated time windows — they were not caused by a WAF regression or by 500s from the backend.

What we found
- AGW access logs (prior 24h) showed 0 HTTP 500 responses from the backend but multiple 502 responses concentrated around 13:46–13:50 UTC, 14:14–14:15 UTC and 14:38–14:51 UTC.  
- AGW firewall logs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching the AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp rule, so the WAF custom rules were allowing expected traffic.  
- The small number of 403s that did appear were expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scan/preview IPs (examples: 146.70.189.237, 52.123.190.89/125), not a new WAF regression.

Conclusion / implication
- The incident was a transient gateway-level failure (AGW returning 502s) or upstream connectivity/transport issue during those windows, not an application 500 error or WAF policy regression.  

(if you want next steps I can list targeted checks to confirm and remediate: probe/health-check logs, AGW resource metrics/events, backend instance/network errors, and Azure platform incident history for those time windows.)

In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. T", "2026-04-14: On 2026-04-14, after new MCP 403s from Janus AWS IPs, added the full Janus AWS address set 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, and 52.40.141.99 to the trusted IP allowlists for fazure MCP and oauth endpoints on Azure Application Gateway. Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set already present in the global policy. Fixed live by updating site policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcent", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, persistent 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/authorize were traced to a mismatch in the global App Gateway WAF allow rule for MCP dynamic client registration and auth paths. AGWAccessLogs in workspace ecae2296-e4c2-47f2-9336-24599caea6be showed repeated GET /authorize requests returning 403 between 2026-03-25T14:40:58Z and 2026-03-25T14:41:34Z. AGWFirewallLogs showed custom rule BlockKnownPath blocking the full RequestUri including query string, and managed rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) plus ", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on ARGS:params.arguments.query with rules 920230, 942100, 942150, 942260, 942360, 942370, 942380, 942410,", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site policy. Result: trusted VPN traffic still traverses OWASP CRS 3.2 managed rules on /mcp and legitima", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding A", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 after expanding the fazure WAF lockdown, a live burst test from Rackspace VPN public IP 72.32.180.181 against https://fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/mcp did not trigger the Application Gateway WAF RateLimit rule. Baseline responses from the VPN client were /health -> 200 and /mcp -> 401. A 130-request burst to /mcp completed with 130 HTTP 401 responses and the immediate follow-up request also returned 401. Log Analytics confirmed 132 /mcp requests with HttpStatus 401 in the 2026-03-19T19:39:00Z minute for ClientIp 72.32.180.181. AGWFirewallLogs for the same client/host/path onl", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, live investigation of App Gateway appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax found two independent exposure paths for fazure-mcp/fazure-oauth. First, the WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax is in Prevention mode but only has Allow rules for RackspaceVPN and JanusAWS; it has no catch-all Block for non-allowed IPs. From external client IP 47.221.164.73, requests to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/health returned 200 and /mcp returned 401 through the gateway, and AGWFirewallLogs showed zero custom rule hits for that client, proving public traffic is allowed unless anot", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Azure-MCP, created and applied dedicated per-site WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites to listeners fazure-mcp, fazure-mcp-dev, fazure-cosmos-mcp, and fazure-cosmos-mcp-dev using script scripts/fazure_per_site_waf.sh. The site policy cloned the global policy managed settings and exclusions, and applied custom rules: RateLimit priority 1 on RequestUri begins with /mcp, VpnOnly priority 2 blocking non-RackspaceVPN IPs, BlockSensitiveFiles priority 4, BlockKnownPath priority 5, GeoBlocking priority 10, AllowSafeFilters pr"]}
